topic: Perplexity Comet Browser Review 2026: AI Browser Worth Switching?

key_points: In-depth review covering interface, AI features, and performance
Notes inconsistent speed compared to Chrome
Highlights one-click summarization as standout feature
Raises privacy concerns over data profiling for ads
14-day Chrome replacement experiment
Found Comet excels at research, summarization, and structured comparisons
Reports agentic tasks fail in real-world scenarios
Concludes it is worth trying for free since there is zero financial risk
30-day long-term user test
Identifies wins in tab organisation and content summarization
Documents frustration when automation breaks on unpredictable websites
Recommends only for power users who treat the browser as a workspace
Describes Comet as the first AI browser that feels like a browser, not a chatbot
Covers agentic capabilities: form-filling, shopping, multi-step tasks across tabs
Warns about prompt injection attack vector (CometJacking)
Notes Chromium base means existing Chrome extensions still work
Honest 30-day review with use-case breakdown
Context-aware assistant reads open tabs saving 15 min/session on long docs
Privacy red flag: CEO stated intent to use browsing context for ad targeting
Compares favorably to Arc Search for research workflows, unfavorably for privacy

stats.fact: Perplexity Comet reached #3 Overall on the iOS App Store within its first week of iOS launch in March 2026, then dropped to Not Ranked amid security controversy.
Comet currently holds 1.9% global browser market share, projected to reach 2.5% by end of 2026.
Comet browser monthly user growth rate is 11.5%, versus Firefox's 3.2%, indicating strong AI browser category momentum.
Perplexity serves 45 million users overall and processes 1.2 to 1.5 billion search queries per month by mid-2026.
Comet went free for all users worldwide on March 23, 2026, originally launched at $200/month for desktop in July 2025.
A federal court ordered Perplexity to halt Comet's AI from making automated purchases on Amazon on March 11, 2026, citing computer fraud allegations.
Zenity Labs researchers discovered vulnerabilities enabling zero-click agent hijacking, local file exfiltration, and password vault takeover within authenticated Comet sessions.
LayerX researchers demonstrated CometJacking where a single malicious URL could hijack Comet AI to siphon Gmail data or execute phishing attacks in under four minutes.
Perplexity Comet is Chromium-based and was released for Windows and macOS on July 9, 2025; Android on November 20, 2025; and iOS on March 18, 2026.
Perplexity holds 6.4% to 8% of the AI chatbot market as of early 2026; approximately 11% of organizations using generative AI tools have adopted Perplexity.

angles: The free AI browser angle: Comet went from $200/month to free — why, and what is the catch? (Answer: ad profiling)
Security disaster angle: How Comet went from #3 on App Store to unranked in two weeks due to CometJacking and security flaws
Amazon legal battle angle: The first agentic browser to face a federal court injunction — and what it means for the future of AI browsers
Privacy trade-off angle: Using Comet means giving Perplexity session-level access to cookies, logins, and open tabs — is that worth it?
Power user vs casual user angle: Comet is transformative for heavy researchers but overkill (and risky) for casual browsing
Chrome replacement test angle: Real-world results of switching from Chrome to Comet for 14 to 30 days
AI browser category definition angle: Comet vs Arc vs Dia vs Chrome — who is actually winning the AI browser race in 2026?
